message:
chore(beads): fix bd-xpgak closed_at < created_at validation error

closed_at was set to 01:55 but the bead was created at 02:00:51 in
the same session. Beads import validation rejected with 'closed_at
cannot be before created_at'. Bumped closed_at to 03:53Z so the
JSONL is internally consistent.

files.patch:
@@ -992,7 +992,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-xejg","title":"socket: execute socket_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs only validates socket fixture metadata even though the shared conformance executor covers socket/bind/listen/shutdown cases. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/socket_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any socket parity regressions exposed by the harness path.","notes":"Closed by cc-libc. Added two test functions to crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/socket_ops_conformance_test.rs:

1. `socket_ops_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case` — dispatches every fixture case through the shared `frankenlibc_fixture_exec::execute_fixture_case` entrypoint and asserts `impl_output == expected_output` + `host_parity` holds. Socket-family executors run impl-only (host_output="SKIP"); parity comes from the RawSyscall path hitting the kernel identically on both sides.
2. `socket_ops_fixture_executes_with_host_parity_via_harness_matrix` — re-runs every case through the `harness conformance-matrix-case` subprocess (same path CI uses), catching packaging / dispatch regressions in the harness binary itself, not just the in-process executor.

No socket parity regressions were exposed. Covered functions from socket_ops.json v1: socket (3 strict + 2 hardened), bind, listen, shutdown, getsockname. All dispatched through the existing execute_socket/bind/listen/shutdown/getsockname_case entrypoints at crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:11109-11175.

Validation: `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test socket_ops_conformance_test` -> 14 passed, 0 failed, 0.14s runtime via rch on vmi1149989.

Also closing bd-ho3il in the same push — already fulfilled by commit 9ea2ad56 which brought fprintf arg-shape coverage to full parity with snprintf; the bead just wasn't marked done.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.315894551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:54:24.295865Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:54:24.295865Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xkc6","title":"POSIX §7.3 conformance table for ctype classification/conversion","description":"ctype::mod has good proptest invariants (prop_core_classification, case_conversion_roundtrip, to_ascii_masks) but no spec-derived table citing POSIX.1-2017 §7.3.1. Add Pattern-4 conformance table with explicit clause refs for is_alpha/is_digit/is_space/is_xdigit/is_cntrl/is_print/is_punct/is_blank and the to_upper/to_lower mapping rules. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/ctype/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:40:33.338358031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.032693512Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:42:33.030520049Z","close_reason":"Added 2 POSIX §7.3 conformance tables in commit 3d7aa71: classification (10 predicates × in/out-of-class sweeps) + case-mapping (full 256-byte sweep asserting alpha mapping + identity on non-alpha). All cases cite IEEE 1003.1-2017 §7.3.1 or §7.3.2 clauses.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xoqs","title":"POSIX services(5) conformance table for parse_services_line","description":"parse_services_line in frankenlibc_core::resolv has smoke-fuzz coverage (bd-s170) and unit tests but no spec-annotated conformance table. Add Pattern-4 conformance table citing services(5) man page clauses — service-name format, port/protocol grammar, aliases, comments. Complements the existing hosts(5) + resolver(5) conformance suite (bd-x7jd).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:24:38.654768153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.989824462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.987850063Z","close_reason":"Added 12-case services(5) conformance table in commit 73bfe8f. Closes the NSS files-backend parser conformance triad: hosts(5) + resolver(5) + services(5), all with spec_ref citations. Complements bd-s170 smoke-fuzz coverage by pinning spec provenance for each documented grammar rule.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id": "bd-xpgak", "title": "xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs", "description": "## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T01:55:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-23T01:55:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean."}
+{"id": "bd-xpgak", "title": "xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs", "description": "## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T03:53:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-23T03:53:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean."}
 {"id":"bd-xsc3","title":"syscall: mount_setattr AT_EMPTY_PATH should not override NULL pathname EINVAL","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_EMPTY_PATH, NULL, 0). Add ABI regression coverage proving AT_EMPTY_PATH does not bypass the NULL pathname contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:46.771233629Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:59:37.158371288Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:59:37.155879079Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xvdk3","title":"syscall: renameat2 null newpath should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, ".", AT_FDCWD, NULL, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the null-destination-path contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:09.402096663Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:07:22.456654477Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:07:22.454530016Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xxd9","title":"bd-25n subtask: Threading/futex lifecycle exhaustive unit+stress test packs","description":"Background:
- Threading and synchronization logic remains a critical defect surface.

Goal:
- Build exhaustive unit test packs for pthread/futex/thread bootstrap lifecycle semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Mutex/condvar/thread/join/detach API-level unit tests.
2) Concurrency adversarial tests with deterministic scheduling controls where feasible.
3) Regression suite for known deadlock/hang signatures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Threading test packs cover success, misuse, contention, timeout, teardown paths.
- Regression signatures are pinned and automatically checked.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit and stress test scripts.
- Structured logs with thread/event timelines and failure markers.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyRiver","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.991066758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:34:54.992244359Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:34:54.992224261Z","close_reason":"Thread/futex lifecycle pack now includes mutex/condvar/rwlock/once/tsd + lifecycle regressions (including self-join). Validated via targeted rch test binaries and workspace quality gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","pthread","testing","unit","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1217,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"DustyRiver","text":"Taking ownership of `bd-xxd9` based on `bv` robot triage.

Triage evidence (robot mode):
- `bv --robot-next` top pick surfaced `bd-rqn` (already in progress).
- `bv --robot-triage` critical path shows `bd-25n` as top impact node, blocked by `bd-xxd9`.
- `bd-25n` currently unblocks 10 downstream issues, so advancing `bd-xxd9` is highest-impact actionable unblock.

Execution start:
- Reassigned `bd-xxd9` to `DustyRiver` and keeping status `in_progress`.
- Starting with release-mode pthread regression signatures already reproduced under LD_PRELOAD:
  - `python3_print` timeout
  - `coreutils_ls_tmp` segfault
  - `sort/wc/redis` timeouts
- Will add deterministic unit+stress coverage and structured artifact references as evidence.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T06:19:58Z"},{"id":1218,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update for `bd-xxd9` (threading/futex lifecycle unit+stress packs):

Implemented in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`:
- Added deterministic stress harness helpers (`env_usize`, `run_create_join_roundtrip_iters`, `run_detach_join_esrch_iters`).
- Converted lifecycle stress tests to bounded defaults to prevent CI hangs:
  - `pthread_create_join_roundtrip_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_ROUNDTRIP_STRESS_ITERS` (default `16`, cap `128`).
  - `pthread_create_join_parallel_batch_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_PARALLEL_BATCH` (default `8`, cap `32`).
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_DETACH_STRESS_ITERS` (default `16`, cap `128`).
- Added explicit opt-in endurance tests (ignored by default):
  - `pthread_create_join_roundtrip_long_stress_profile`
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_long_stress_profile`

Validation run (CPU-intensive cargo via `rch`):
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`8 passed, 2 ignored`)
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed`)
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Notes:
- `rch` remote daemon intermittently timed out in this environment; fail-open local execution was used where needed (per AGENTS.md behavior).
- `bd-xxd9` remains `in_progress`; next step is extending regression signatures toward known LD_PRELOAD deadlock/timeout patterns while preserving deterministic runtimes.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T06:43:30Z"},{"id":1219,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Additional `bd-xxd9` progress update:

What I advanced in this pass:
- Added ABI-level condvar timedwait argument-validation regression in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_cond_core_test.rs`:
  - `condvar_timedwait_rejects_null_abstime_and_invalid_nsec`
- Added lifecycle handle-consumption misuse regression in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`:
  - `pthread_join_then_reuse_handle_is_esrch`

Stability hardening for lifecycle stress coverage:
- Converted default stress entries to explicit opt-in (`#[ignore]`) with clear labels to avoid wedging default test runs under known native-threading flake windows:
  - `pthread_create_join_roundtrip_stress`
  - `pthread_create_join_parallel_batch_stress`
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_stress`
- Long-run stress variants remain ignored and available for explicit endurance runs.

New discovered defect (tracked separately):
- Created child bug bead `bd-xxd9.1`:
  - "Native pthread self-join can deadlock under thread lifecycle path"
  - Repro came from a self-join regression attempt that could wedge the lifecycle suite.

Validation (via `rch exec -- ...`):
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed, 5 ignored`)
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`7 passed`)
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅ (remote build succeeded; artifact retrieval path was flaky)
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅ (remote build succeeded; artifact retrieval path was flaky)
","created_at":"2026-02-16T07:05:11Z"},{"id":1220,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Subtask progress: advanced  self-join deadlock hardening with code changes in core thread lifecycle + ABI join guard and validated lifecycle/condvar suites.  remains in_progress while we finish deterministic self-join coverage so regression can be unignored safely.","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:07:12Z"},{"id":1221,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Subtask progress: advanced `bd-xxd9.1` self-join deadlock hardening with core lifecycle + ABI join-entry guards and re-validated lifecycle/condvar suites.

Current state:
- Parent `bd-xxd9` remains in progress.
- Child bug `bd-xxd9.1` remains in progress while we finish deterministic startup-window handling needed to unignore the self-join regression probe.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:08:28Z"},{"id":1222,"issue_id":"bd-xxd9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update:
- Closed child bug `bd-xxd9.1` after re-enabling `pthread_self_join_is_rejected_with_edeadlk` in default lifecycle suite.
- Validation evidence:
  - repeated explicit runs of self-join probe (`--ignored`) passed 3/3 via `rch`
  - default lifecycle suite now includes self-join test and passed: `7 passed, 5 ignored`
  - condvar suite passed: `7 passed`
  - workspace gates passed: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

`bd-xxd9` remains in-progress for broader threading/futex lifecycle pack completion beyond this self-join defect.
","created_at":"2026-02-16T14:31:41Z"}]}
